coalition_note: The Borderlands coalition currently has 40 members. The contact table below shows a small set of PLACEHOLDER sample entries (named after native Pacific Northwest flora and fauna) to demonstrate how the data pipeline structures and cleans contact data — it is not the full roster.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Intergenerational Cooking Circles
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: A Native grandmother and a non-Indigenous grandmother cook together and exchange ancestral food stories.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Child-Led Storytelling Hikes
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: Connect land, imagination, nature literacy, and place-based learning.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Elder–Youth Roundtables
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: Indigenous and non-Indigenous elders and youth share memory and relational knowledge across generations.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Rewilding a Public Plot
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: Community teams tend, observe, and document Indigenous planting methods over time.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Animated Indigenous Legends
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: Screenings of animated Indigenous stories followed by facilitated question circles for children and families.

learning_lab_pilot_concepts.title: Youth-Designed Cultural Celebrations
learning_lab_pilot_concepts.desc: Youth-designed, adult-supported events centering cross-cultural exchange and joyful connection.

physical_locations.name: Beaver Tales Coffee / swədəbš Cultural Center

goals: Protect and uplift Indigenous knowledge and ways of knowing
Use storytelling as a catalyst for transformation
Build collective capacity for social and political action
Regenerate connection to people, place, and land
